description_long: This object consists of three wooden picnic tables, each with attached benches on either side. The tables are rectangular with slatted surfaces, and the benches are constructed from matching wooden planks, supported by sturdy cross-braced legs. The wood appears to be finished with a natural brown stain, giving a warm and inviting appearance suitable for communal outdoor use. The tables are arranged in a row, each with a slightly different design, but all share a similar construction style and proportions. The benches are integrated into the table structure, providing stable seating for multiple people. There are no visible umbrella holes or additional accessories on the tabletops.
description: A set of three wooden picnic tables with integrated benches, featuring slatted surfaces and cross-braced legs in a natural brown finish.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the slatted surfaces of the three tables and the arrangement of benches on either side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the height, thickness of the tabletops, and the cross-braced support structure beneath each table.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view emphasizing the underside construction, including the cross-bracing and the alignment of the benches.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, showing the same features from the reverse angle and the uniformity of the bench attachments.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: Each tabletop is supported by robust wooden legs arranged in an A-frame or cross-braced configuration, providing stability and strength. The legs are connected by horizontal beams that reinforce the structure and support the benches.
